purpose: Defines the !initialize slash command — one-time setup of Synapse for a target project. Eight-step workflow: (1) verify Synapse install + project root exist, (2) detect tech stack from package.json/tsconfig/next.config/firebase.json/requirements.txt/go.mod/Cargo.toml/etc. AND detect monorepo type (npm-workspaces/pnpm/lerna/nx/turbo/cargo/go) by reading workspace fields and resolving glob patterns to package directories, (3) create {project_root}/.synapse/ with toc.md placeholder, config.json (containing project_name, tech_stack, tracker_root, pki_path, monorepo packages list), and knowledge/{annotations,queries}/ subdirs, (4) audit/scaffold CLAUDE.md (only if absent), (5) generate TOC via !toc_generate (parallel swarm — most time-consuming step, supports monorepo package grouping), (6) ensure {tracker_root}/dashboards|Archive|history|queue|tasks|conversations/ directories exist, (7) start the dashboard server via !start, (8) print final report with Next Steps. Supports --skip-toc and --skip-claude flags.
gotchas: Idempotent where possible — running !initialize twice should detect existing state and skip already-completed steps, never break.
NEVER overwrite existing CLAUDE.md — scaffolding only creates new files for projects that have none.
NEVER overwrite existing .synapse/toc.md if it has content — skip TOC generation unless user explicitly asks.
Dashboards are created DYNAMICALLY on demand with 6-character hex IDs (e.g., a3f7k2) by nextDashboardId() — there are NO fixed slots. Only the dashboards/ directory itself is created here.
The reserved 'ide' dashboard is auto-created by Electron for IDE chat views — it cannot be deleted and is never used by swarm agents.

Monorepo detection drives TOC organization: when monorepo detected, .synapse/config.json's monorepo field is populated and !toc_generate organizes by package (## @myorg/api/, ## @myorg/web/) rather than flat directory listing.
If project is NOT a monorepo, monorepo field in config.json must be explicitly null — not omitted.
Step 5 (TOC generation) is the only non-serial step — it delegates to !toc_generate's parallel swarm.
Only abort for prerequisite failures (Steps 1a-1b); other failures (TOC generation, server start) are warnings — initialization continues.
patterns: Sequential phase pipeline: verify → detect → create → scaffold → generate → infrastructure → start → report.
Monorepo detection: read workspace field → resolve globs → enumerate packages → store in config.
Idempotent setup with explicit warnings on re-run.
conventions: Modifies filesystem — every modification is reported to the user.
OK / Warning / X status icons in output tables.
